pricing_shape: Free developer plan, $49/month production plan, usage/log limits, overage per additional 100k requests, custom enterprise, and open-source self-host option.
published_anchor: Production plan listed at $49/month with 100k recorded logs/month and overage pricing.
kolm_pricing_implication: Gateway control is priced cheaply enough that Kolm should not anchor on generic gateway access.
risk: Bundle gateway import/routing as integration, not the core paid unit.

pricing_shape: Open-source proxy plus enterprise SaaS/self-managed features sold via contact-sales motion.
published_anchor: Enterprise page emphasizes SSO, SCIM, OIDC/JWT auth, support, admin delegation, guardrails, and monitoring; no public seat/usage price found in checked page.
kolm_pricing_implication: Open-source proxy economics make gateway replacement a weak monetization story.
risk: Use LiteLLM as an integration path and charge for artifact governance/registry.

pricing_shape: Core gateway features free on all plans; persistent logs included with plan limits; premium/Workers-related add-ons for Logpush, guardrails, and platform services.
published_anchor: Core features such as analytics, caching, and rate limiting are described as free; Workers Paid includes larger log limits and Logpush pricing.
kolm_pricing_implication: Cloudflare can underprice AI gateway basics through bundled infrastructure.
risk: Kolm should avoid charging for proxy basics and instead monetize compiler, receipts, registry, and governance.

pricing_shape: Pay-as-you-go credits, $5/month free credits on team accounts, no model markup, BYOK no markup or gateway fee.
published_anchor: Vercel docs state paid tier is pay-as-you-go and provider list prices are used with no markup.
kolm_pricing_implication: Vercel sets expectation that gateway access itself is near pass-through.
risk: Build Vercel AI SDK examples and avoid percent-of-token-spend pricing.

pricing_shape: Free plan, pay-as-you-go credits, 5.5 percent platform fee on credits, 5 percent BYOK fee after first 1M BYOK requests/month, enterprise custom.
published_anchor: Pay-as-you-go shows 5.5 percent platform fee; BYOK docs describe 1M free BYOK requests/month then 5 percent fee.
kolm_pricing_implication: Routing marketplaces can monetize convenience but keep inference prices transparent and low-friction.
risk: Kolm should not depend on markup economics; charge for artifacts, private registry, proof retention, and org controls.

pricing_shape: Free hobby plan, $79/month Pro, $799/month Team, enterprise custom, request/storage usage-based pricing.
published_anchor: Pro listed at $79/month; Team at $799/month; free includes 10k requests and 1GB storage.
kolm_pricing_implication: Observability vendors price seats/orgs plus request/storage usage.
risk: Kolm should avoid pure trace storage pricing unless tied to compile candidates and receipts.

pricing_shape: Free Hobby plan, $29/month Core, $199/month Pro, $2499/month Enterprise, units-based overages and retention/security tiers.
published_anchor: Core listed at $29/month; Pro $199/month; Enterprise $2499/month; unit overages around $8/100k with volume steps.
kolm_pricing_implication: Open-source plus low cloud entry pricing makes generic observability hard to monetize.
risk: Charge for artifact lifecycle/governance and keep trace ingestion compatible with Langfuse.

pricing_shape: Open Source self-hosting free for core features; Enterprise self-host custom with ClickHouse Cloud/BYOC/Private and enterprise controls.
published_anchor: Self-hosted Open Source tier is free; Enterprise is custom pricing with support/security features.
kolm_pricing_implication: Self-hosted observability reduces willingness to pay for another hosted trace store.
risk: Make self-hosted Kolm registry/receipt verifier clear if enterprise buyers need data control.

pricing_shape: Starter free, Pro monthly plan, Enterprise custom; usage grows with processed data and higher limits/security.
published_anchor: Official plans page lists Starter free and Pro at $249/month.
kolm_pricing_implication: Braintrust owns paid eval/observability workflow at a mid-market SaaS price.
risk: Kolm K-score must be artifact acceptance/proof, not a generic eval dashboard substitute.

pricing_shape: Free plan with request/log-retention limit, Pro per-user monthly plan, advanced features such as evals/fine-tuning/workspaces.
published_anchor: Docs page lists Pro at $50/month/user and Free with 5k requests/7 days retention.
kolm_pricing_implication: Prompt management is seat-priced and crowded.
risk: Kolm should consume prompt versions and charge for compiled artifact release governance.

pricing_shape: Free tier plus usage-based private serverless GPU pricing, shared serverless token allowances, VPC custom quotes, fine-tuning per 1M tokens by model size/type.
published_anchor: Private serverless examples include L4/A10G/L40S/A100 hourly rates; fine-tuning listed per 1M tokens for LoRA/Turbo/RFT categories.
kolm_pricing_implication: Fine-tuning platforms monetize compute and managed model serving.
risk: Kolm should not price as fine-tuning infra until model-adapter tier is real.

pricing_shape: Serverless per-token inference, dedicated GPU hourly rates, GPU clusters, sandbox/code interpreter pricing, fine-tuning per 1M processed tokens by model size/type.
published_anchor: Fine-tuning listed per 1M tokens; dedicated inference examples include H100/H200/B200 hourly pricing.
kolm_pricing_implication: Together exposes raw compute/token economics that buyers can compare directly.
risk: Kolm pricing should avoid GPU/token claims unless it controls that runtime.

pricing_shape: Gateway is positioned as beta with unified API and 0 percent markup language; production observability remains the standard integration path.
published_anchor: Docs describe AI Gateway as beta and standard integrations as production-ready observability.
kolm_pricing_implication: Even observability vendors are adding low-markup gateways as an acquisition surface.
risk: Kolm should use gateways as capture surfaces and avoid making gateway beta features central.

pricing_shape: Marketplace advertises broad model/provider catalog and high usage; pricing is transparent by model/provider plus platform/BYOK fees.
published_anchor: Home page advertises 400+ models, 60+ providers, and large token/user scale; provider page lists BYOK/data policy attributes by provider.
kolm_pricing_implication: Routing scale and model catalog are not realistic first moats for Kolm.
risk: Use model marketplaces for fallback/runtime access while Kolm owns artifact proof.
